cards.definition: the weighted average mass of an atom of an element compared to 1/12th the mass of a carbon-12 atom
the sum of the relative atomic masses of all atoms in a formula — used in mole calculations
a unit measuring amount of substance — one mole contains 6.02 × 10²³ particles (Avogadro's number)
the amount of solute dissolved in a given volume of solution — measured in mol/dm³ or g/dm³
the region around the nucleus where electrons are found — electrons fill shells from the innermost outward
an equation showing only the ions that change during a reaction — spectator ions are omitted
the formation of an insoluble solid when two solutions are mixed — used to identify ions in solution
a separation technique used to identify substances in a mixture — components travel different distances based on their properties
a technique to find the concentration of an acid or alkali by carefully adding a known solution until neutralisation occurs
the amount of product obtained in a reaction — percentage yield = (actual yield ÷ theoretical yield) × 100
how quickly a reaction occurs — measured by the change in amount of reactant or product per unit time
reactions occur when particles collide with sufficient energy — increasing temperature, concentration, or surface area increases the rate
in a closed system, the forward and reverse reactions occur at equal rates — concentrations remain constant
if conditions change in a system at equilibrium, the system shifts to oppose the change
a saturated hydrocarbon with only single C-C bonds — general formula CₙH₂ₙ₊₂. E.g. methane (CH₄), ethane (C₂H₆)
an unsaturated hydrocarbon containing a C=C double bond — general formula CₙH₂ₙ. More reactive than alkanes
a reaction where atoms add across a double bond — e.g. ethene + bromine → dibromoethane
joining many small monomer molecules together to form a long polymer chain — e.g. ethene → polyethene
an analysis of the environmental impact of a product throughout its entire life — from raw material extraction to disposal
a measure of how efficiently atoms in reactants are converted into useful products — higher is more sustainable
